data.research_question: How do concurrency, workspace scale, command work, and session lifecycle affect command execution? How do concurrency, payload, topology, source, and mutation destination affect file operations? How do workspace scale, network profile, and concurrent session count affect time to ready? How do storage topology and live-session load affect squash, commit, and remount behavior?

factors.help: Fixture request for the share of live sessions eligible to migrate, rounded to the nearest whole session with halves upward; observed M remains evidence.

factors.label: Remount parallelism W
factors.help: Fixed bounded width of the live-session remount sweep inside the single squash request.

factors.label: Layers per block
factors.help: Published layers in each squashable block of the prepared topology.

factors.label: Payload bytes

factors.label: Session activity
factors.help: Whether prepared live sessions remain idle or perform bounded deterministic activity before squash.

factors.label: Session activity

methods.failure_policy: only measured product-success, required-check-pass, cleanup-restored trials are eligible
methods.resource_policy: availability is explicit; unavailable samples are never converted to zero
methods.comparison_policy: compatibility_before_delta; independent bootstrap; no p-value or v1 regression verdict

limitations: P95 is exploratory below 20 successful observations; P99 is not computed in v1.
Median confidence intervals are omitted below five successful observations.
Unavailable resource evidence remains unavailable and cannot be interpreted as zero.
V1 reports direction metadata but makes no automatic regression or significance claim.
